questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must a Utah sales agent candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Utah requires 120 classroom hours of salesperson prelicensing education covering Principles & Practices and Utah License Law. This education must be completed within one year prior to licensing.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed pre-license education 14 months ago but has not yet taken the exam. What is the status of their education?
questions.correct_answer: It has expired and must be retaken
distractors: Only the state portion must be retaken
questions.explanation: Pre-license education in Utah expires after 12 months. Since 14 months have passed, the candidate must complete the education again before being eligible to take the exam.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a real estate sales agent license in Utah?
questions.explanation: Utah requires candidates to be at least 18 years old and have a high school diploma or GED to be eligible for a sales agent license.

questions.question_text: A candidate arrives at the Pearson VUE test center for the Utah sales agent exam. What document must they present from their school?
questions.explanation: Candidates must present the Candidate Education Certifying Document, which is stamped and signed by the approved school, verifying completion of the required 120 hours of pre-license education.

questions.question_text: As part of the Utah licensing process, candidates must submit to a:
questions.explanation: Utah requires all license candidates to complete a criminal background check via fingerprinting. This helps ensure that individuals entering the profession meet character and fitness standards.

questions.question_text: After passing both sections of the Utah licensing exam, a candidate has how long to apply for a license?
questions.explanation: A candidate must apply for their license within 90 days of passing both the general and state exam components. If they fail to apply within this window, they may need to retake the exam.

questions.question_text: A candidate passed the general portion of the exam in January but failed the state portion. They retake and pass the state portion in April. When does the 90-day application clock start?
questions.correct_answer: From the date both portions are passed
distractors: From the first exam attempt date
From the general portion pass date
questions.explanation: The 90-day application deadline starts when both exam components (general and state) have been passed. The candidate must also complete the entire process within 12 months of their education completion.

questions.question_text: The total time allowed for both sections of the Utah salesperson exam is:
questions.explanation: Candidates are given 4 hours total for both the national/general and state sections combined. The exam is administered electronically at Pearson VUE test centers with immediate scoring.

questions.question_text: What passing score is required on both portions of the Utah salesperson exam?
questions.explanation: Utah salesperson candidates must achieve a 70 scaled score on both the general and state portions. The scaled score is statistically adjusted and is not the same as a raw percentage correct.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed education in March and must finish the entire licensing process by when?
questions.explanation: The exam and licensing process must be completed within 12 months of education completion. Education completed in March expires the following March, so all steps must be finished before then.

questions.question_text: Which person is exempt from Utah real estate licensing requirements?
questions.correct_answer: An attorney acting within their practice
distractors: A property owner's friend helping for a fee
A part-time negotiator paid per deal
questions.explanation: Active attorneys acting within the scope of their legal practice are exempt from licensing. Other exemptions include persons acting under court order and government employees performing official duties.

questions.question_text: A court appoints a receiver to sell real property from an estate. Does the receiver need a real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: No, persons acting under court order are exempt
distractors: Yes, all property sellers need a license
Only if they receive a commission
questions.explanation: Persons acting under a court order are exempt from Utah licensing requirements. This includes court-appointed receivers, trustees, and executors selling property as ordered by the court.

questions.question_text: A county employee negotiates the purchase of land for a public highway project as part of their official duties. Do they need a license?
questions.correct_answer: No, government employees in official duties are exempt
distractors: Yes, they are performing a real estate act
Only if the purchase exceeds $1 million
Only if they receive extra compensation
questions.explanation: Government employees performing real estate activities as part of their official duties are exempt from licensing. This applies to actions taken in the course of their government employment, not personal transactions.

questions.question_text: An active member of the Utah State Bar wants to become a sales agent. What advantage do they have?
questions.correct_answer: They may have education requirements waived
distractors: They are automatically licensed as a broker
They skip the state portion of the exam
They receive a discounted exam fee
questions.explanation: Active Utah State Bar members may have the pre-license education requirement waived. However, they must still pass the licensing exam and complete the application process.

questions.question_text: A neighbor helps sell a friend's property and accepts a $500 gift in appreciation. Under Utah law, this person:
distractors: Is legal if it was a one-time act
questions.explanation: Utah's 'one act for compensation' rule means anyone performing a real estate act for compensation who is not exempt must hold a license. A gift for real estate services constitutes compensation regardless of the amount.
